A_intro:
When an agricultural background or education in agriculture is a necessary part
of training for those who have entered, or are planning to enter an agricultural
occupation, it becomes the responsibility of agricultural education to provide this
education. ;
The following types of programs will be suggested as offerings in public schools
to meet the needs of education in agriculture:
I. EXPLORATORY AGRICULTURE
To explore agriculture at the elementary;,:junior high and high school level.
We will work with guidance people in promoting this program.
II. VOCATIONAL AGRICULTURE
To prepare youth, young farmers and adult farmers in public schools for farming and for agricultural occupations which require competence in farming.
TIL. GENERAL AGRICULTURE
To meet the needs of boys and girls in high school who have an interest in
or can profit from a knowledge of agriculture.
IV. AGRICULIURE SERVICE OCCUPATIONS
To meet the needs of boys, girls, and adults in high school, post high school
and junior college, who have entered, or are preparing to enter agricultural
service occupations,
Cooperative programs will be encouraged with other services where possible.
V. FARM EMPLOYMENT
To meet the needs of youth and adults for employment on farms.
The Committee on Types of Programs in the Pacific Region recommend that. the U.S.
Office implement a program in agricultural education for migrant farm workers. It is
A_methods:
further recommended that this program be conducted under the State Staff of agri-
cultural education and in cooperation with teachers of vocational agriculture when
possible.
-27-.
Course Content
States of the region are encouraged to investigate the areas listed below and
to develop pilot programs in the areas which are applicable to needs. Examples of
the content for the various types of courses are shown in the appended chart.
I. EXPLORATORY AGRICULTURE
Agricultural education in this area shall encompass the development. of
appreciations and understandings as presented at the elementary and junior
high school levels. Examples of types of courses in this area are pre-
sented in the appended chart.
II. VOCATIONAL AGRICULTURE
High School Level
It is recommended that worthwhile programs be maintained under
existing procedures of program planning.
Young Farmer Level
Adult Farmer Level
III. GENERAL AGRICULTURE (Agricultural Service, Agricultural Arts, etc.)
A_results:
Agricultural education in this area will encompass the study of
agriculture as general education for all citizens.
Iv. AGRICULTURAT. SERVICE OCCUPATIONS
This area will encompass the education in agriculture for agricultural
service occupations, presented at the senior high school and post high
school levels.
Vv. FARM EMPLOYMENT
This area will encourage the education for on-the-farm employment, other
than vocational agriculture, presented at the senior high school and post
high school levels and for high school drop-outs.
